name: Fionn mac Cumhaill
description: Fionn mac Cumhaill (pronounced 'Finn McCool') is a central figure in Irish mythology, famed as a warrior, hunter, and leader of the Fianna, a band of elite warriors. Born Deimne, he gained the name Fionn ('fair' or 'white') after an incident where he burned his thumb while cooking the Salmon of Knowledge and instinctively put it in his mouth. This act granted him immense wisdom and foresight. His father, Cumhall, was the leader of the Fianna before being slain, and Fionn spent his youth in hiding, learning the ways of the warrior and druidry. He eventually avenged his father's death and assumed leadership of the Fianna, leading them through numerous battles and adventures. Fionn is known for his exceptional strength, agility, and strategic mind, as well as his close relationship with the natural world. He is associated with many landmarks in Ireland, often attributed to his legendary feats. His tales are filled with magic, heroism, and encounters with mythical creatures, making him a powerful symbol of Irish identity and a beloved figure in Irish folklore. Fionn's sagas represent the ideals of chivalry, courage, and wisdom, inspiring generations with his legendary exploits and profound connection to the land. He is one of the most important figures in the Mythological Cycle of Irish Mythology.
subtitle: Legendary Leader of the Fianna
achievements: Building the Giant's Causeway (in some versions)

keyMyths.title: The Salmon of Knowledge
keyMyths.description: Fionn accidentally gained immense wisdom by burning his thumb while cooking the Salmon of Knowledge for his master, Finnegas, and instinctively putting his thumb in his mouth. This act fulfilled the prophecy and granted him the salmon's power.

keyMyths.title: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne
keyMyths.description: Fionn was betrothed to Gráinne, but she eloped with Diarmuid Ua Duibhne. Fionn pursued them relentlessly, leading to a tragic tale of love, betrayal, and ultimately, Diarmuid's death.
keyMyths.source: The Pursuit of Diarmuid and Gráinne

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Gabhra
keyMyths.description: This battle marked the decline of the Fianna and the end of their era of glory. It involved a conflict between the Fianna and the High King, resulting in heavy losses for both sides.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: A popular retelling of Irish myths and legends.
sources.text: A popular retelling of Irish myths and legends.

sources.author: T.W. Rolleston
sources.work: Myths and Legends of the Celtic Race
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Celtic mythology.
